dataSourceNote: Slack: all #mon-prosp-* channels + #prosp-monarch-deals. Granola: CLS Health meeting notes found (5/5 demo). No Granola data for other prospects. No Gong access.

executiveSummaries.size: 4 locs (2 pilot), ~2K calls/week; free pilot → $1.25/resolved call + $0.40/ClearPay at scale
executiveSummaries.status: Signed 4/27. Channel renamed mon-partner-gloc on 5/1 — deal is live and in implementation. Kickoff underway: Carli (ClearPay) collecting Athena API credentials and requesting a preview environment copy-down; Kennedy (Maya) gathering phone routing specs and DID requirements. Kapeesh requested a Google Drive SOP to document design partner onboarding learnings for future customers/agents.
keyRisks: Resolution rate unproven in ortho — free pilot pricing locked before we know the numbers
Jim signed without a reference customer; delivery must prove the model
DID phone line may not exist at GLOC yet — provisioning takes 24-96 hours
executiveSummaries.nextStep: GLOC to provide Athena credentials + complete 3rd-party auth form; Kennedy to confirm DID status — Owner: Carli/Kennedy — Due: ASAP

executiveSummaries.size: $3,500/month (up to 3K telemed visits) + 1% SolvPay + $0.50/ClearPay; 15–30 telehealth physicians, 250-300+ providers total (Texas/Houston)
executiveSummaries.champion: Abigail Escalante (EMR Ops Manager), Dr. Khaled Attia (clinical); Dr. Baba (decision-maker, skeptical); President of CLS engaged and driving urgency
executiveSummaries.status: Full telehealth demo delivered 5/5 (Granola + Slack confirmed). Dr. Baba is the decision-maker — engaged but explicitly gating on ECW integration proof: 'if this doesn't integrate with ECW, let us know.' HMO/PCP routing logic unresolved (workaround proposed: place of service code 20 or cash pay). Pricing shared ($3,500/month flat); CLS pushing for hybrid model (smaller platform fee + usage-based), 6-month first term, and waived $2K impl fee. Luma disqualified by CLS — confirmed unable to match dynamic eligibility. President wants this to potentially replace Luma for ALL primary care scheduling, a major scope expansion. CLS is committing a full-time project resource. Target go-live June 1; implementation 6-8 weeks means August 1 worst case.
keyRisks: ECW integration proof is the hard gate — Dr. Baba needs a demo video or reference (ECW + multi-provider + Texas preferred), not assurances
HMO/assigned PCP billing workaround unresolved — could block a meaningful patient segment at launch
Pricing negotiation unresolved — CLS wants hybrid pricing, shorter term, and waived impl fee simultaneously
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Deliver ECW integration proof (video or ECW + multi-provider + Texas reference) — Owner: Brantley/Danny — Due: 1-2 days

executiveSummaries.size: 3 existing locs + 1 standalone ortho urgent care opening June 1; MRR TBD
executiveSummaries.champion: Jennifer Hazelton (Dir. of Marketing, ex-PhysicianOne/Solv user), Christopher LaFave (Executive Director)
executiveSummaries.status: Strong 4/8 full executive demo — Executive Director, Ops Manager, Clinical Manager, and Dir. of Marketing all attended and asked the right operational questions. Team confirmed urgent ortho workflow fit; verbal interest given. Jennifer is already sold; June 1 new location opening is the forcing function. Christopher pushed for a live ECW data-flow demo before committing. Team agreed to internal review with a reconnect in ~1 week from 4/8 (that reconnect may have slipped).
keyRisks: ECW integration confidence is a hard gate — Christopher needs to see data actually flowing into ECW
Referral workflow gap may block expansion to the 3 non-urgent ortho locations
Deck + ECW integration packet delivery appears overdue from week of 4/8 — risk of momentum loss
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Brantley to confirm follow-up call is scheduled and send ECW integration packet — Owner: Brantley — Due: Overdue (was Mon/Tues week of 4/14), chase immediately

executiveSummaries.champion: Rikin Shah (Chief of Staff) — not final DM
executiveSummaries.status: As of 4/7, effectively cut off. Rikin won't replace their CTO-built scheduler ('too many resources poured in') and is 6-9 months out from any real timeline. Only viable path is Solv as a patient acquisition layer (GMB routes to Solv booking alongside their existing scheduler) — a non-standard deal structure with unknown internal appetite. Rikin is also piloting a competing voice agent and expressed skepticism about Maya. Teresa and Kapeesh are aware — team has the option to close this out.
keyRisks: 6-9 month timeline — no realistic path to a 2026 deal
Competing voice agent in active pilot — Maya entry closed for now
Non-standard acquisition-only deal structure requires internal Solv go/no-go
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Internal Solv decision: pursue acquisition-only model or close out — Owner: Teresa/Kapeesh — Due: TBD

executiveSummaries.status: Paused since 3/11 due to unresolved Assort Health contract exit. Jon proactively communicated the block rather than ghosting and confirmed intent to return to Solv when clear — no timeline given. This deal was near-signature in late February before the Assort issue surfaced. Last touch was 3/30 (friendly, no asks). Bryan joined the channel on 4/22 for monitoring. Passive cadence only.
keyRisks: Zero visibility into Assort exit timeline — could be months
Deal was near-signature; restarting after a long pause will require re-engagement
Competitive risk grows during the pause window
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Passive monitoring — periodic check-in every 2 weeks; next touch ~mid-May — Owner: Brantley

executiveSummaries.champion: Randy Johansen (Board Member, former AFC CEO) — intro via Heather at UCA happy hour
executiveSummaries.status: Note: Based on #prosp-monarch-deals only — no dedicated channel or Granola data found. Very early. First exploratory call 4/24. Org opened 3 locations simultaneously and cash-constrained itself — Kennesaw (Atlanta) location is a serious drag (opened months without an MRI). Randy is meeting with the OS1 team to assess if they're in a position to evaluate solutions. Reconnect planned for Friday/Monday week of 4/28. Brantley to share ClearPay + Maya materials with Evan Cory's help.
keyRisks: Cash constraints may make any new spend a non-starter before operations stabilize
Randy is a board member, not an operator — unclear internal decision authority
No EHR or operational contacts identified yet
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Share ClearPay + Maya materials with Randy for OS1 team meeting — Owner: Brantley/Evan — Due: Week of 4/28 (may be slightly overdue, confirm status)

executiveSummaries.champion: Katia Sarfian (Administrator) — owner Dr. Tovmassian is the blocker
executiveSummaries.status: Note: ClearPay-only deal — not a Monarch bundle. Near-dead. Dr. Tovmassian (owner, also owns existing Solv partner Mederi) rejected ClearPay on 3/16: 'doesn't make sense for our practice, 90% of patients are recurring.' Product-market fit genuinely doesn't exist for primary care under current ClearPay pricing. No response to 3/24 follow-up. Not pushing hard to protect the existing Mederi relationship.
keyRisks: Owner-level objection is substantive — recurring primary care patient model doesn't fit current ClearPay pricing
Pushing too hard risks damaging the existing Mederi partner relationship
executiveSummaries.nextStep: Low-priority dormant follow-up; revisit when ClearPay evolves a primary care use case — Owner: Brantley — Due: No near-term action

leadershipTable.keyNotes: Signed 4/27; implementation live — Athena API access + Maya DID setup in progress.
5/5 demo done; ECW integration proof is the hard gate — Dr. Baba needs video/reference in 1-2 days.
Strong 4/8 exec demo; ECW live-demo + deck delivery overdue — June 1 is forcing function.
6-9 month timeline; won't replace homegrown scheduler; competing voice agent in pilot — need go/no-go.
Blocked by Assort Health contract exit since 3/11; COO will re-engage when resolved.
Board-level intro only; cash-strapped org evaluating capacity to buy after overexpanding.
Owner rejected ClearPay outright — primary care recurring-patient model isn't a fit; not Monarch.

closedLost.reason: Ghosted — no response after multiple follow-ups since March 9. Admin contact (Osvaldo) was bought in but providers were the real decision-makers and never engaged. Closed out by Brantley 4/7.
Deal broken off by Solv — Dr. Chrono EHR incompatibility made ClearPay integration infeasible. Pricing model also mismatched (2 rotating providers across 6 locations vs. per-location pricing).
